activities_projects.title: Mughal Family Tree
activities_projects.objective: Identify the main Mughal emperors and their lineage
steps: List the two lineages of the Mughals: Genghis Khan (mother's side) and Timur (father's side)
Explain why the Mughals preferred to be called 'Timurids' instead of 'Mongols'
Draw a timeline of the six 'Great Mughals' from Babur to Aurangzeb
Write the years each emperor ruled
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the ancestry and chronological order of the major Mughal rulers
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the timeline and lineage names (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Babur and the First Battle of Panipat
activities_projects.objective: Understand how the Mughal Empire began in India
steps: Identify the year Babur defeated Ibrahim Lodi (1526)
Name the place where this famous battle happened (Panipat)
List one new technology Babur used in the battle (Canvons/Gunpowder)
Write down that Babur was the first Mughal Emperor
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the key event and technology that led to the foundation of the Mughal Empire
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct facts about 1526 and the use of artillery (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mansabdar and Zat
activities_projects.objective: Understand the ranking system in the Mughal army
steps: Define 'Mansabdar' (an individual who holds a rank/position)
Identify 'Zat' as the numerical value that decided the rank and salary
Note that the higher the Zat, the more prestigious the noble's position
Explain that Mansabdars had to maintain a specific number of sawar (cavalrymen)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Grasps the basic structure of the Mughal administrative and military ranking system
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct definition of Mansab, Zat, and Sawar (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Zabt and Zamindars
activities_projects.objective: Learn how the Mughals collected taxes
steps: Identify 'Zamindars' as the intermediaries who collected land revenue
Define 'Zabt' as the revenue system based on careful survey of crop yields and prices
Name Akbar's revenue minister who developed this system (Todar Mal)
List one reason why the Mughals needed this money (to pay the army and build monuments)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the importance of land revenue and the role of Todar Mal in Mughal economics
activities_projects.evaluation: Identification of Todar Mal and the definition of Zabt (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Akbar Nama and Ain-i Akbari
activities_projects.objective: Identify the source of information for Akbar's reign
steps: Identify the author of 'Akbar Nama' (Abul Fazl)
Learn that the book has three volumes, and the third volume is called 'Ain-i Akbari'
Describe what 'Ain-i Akbari' deals with (administration, army, revenues, and geography)
Draw a book cover for 'Ain-i Akbari'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes Abul Fazl’s work as a primary source of Mughal history
activities_projects.evaluation: Knowledge of the author and the contents of the third volume (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sulh-i Kul: Universal Peace
steps: Define 'Sulh-i Kul' (Universal Peace)
Explain that this idea did not discriminate between people of different religions
Note that it focused on honesty, justice, and peace
Discuss why Akbar held discussions in the 'Ibadat Khana' with people of different faiths
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains Akbar’s vision of a tolerant and inclusive empire
activities_projects.evaluation: Understanding of the term Sulh-i Kul and its core values (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Big Five: Mughal Monuments
activities_projects.objective: Identify the architectural achievements of the Mughals
materials: Pictures of Humayun's Tomb, Red Fort, Taj Mahal, Jama Masjid, Fatehpur Sikri
steps: Match the monument with the Emperor who built it
Identify the 'Red Fort' and 'Taj Mahal' as creations of Shah Jahan
List the main material used (Red Sandstone or White Marble)
Write one sentence on why these buildings are famous today
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects specific Mughal rulers with their architectural legacy
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct matching of rulers and monuments (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Jagir and Jagirdar
steps: Define 'Jagir' (land assignments given to Mansabdars instead of cash salary)
Explain that 'Jagirdars' collected revenue from these lands
Note that unlike Muqtis, Jagirdars did not always live in or administer their Jagirs
Explain that the Emperor's officials checked if the revenue collected was correct
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between the Iqta system of Sultans and the Jagir system of Mughals
activities_projects.evaluation: Definition of Jagir and the role of the Jagirdar (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Nur Jahan’s Influence
activities_projects.objective: Recognize the role of women in the Mughal court
steps: Identify Nur Jahan as the wife of Emperor Jahangir
List two ways she showed her power (coins in her name, royal seals/farman)
Describe how she remained extremely loyal and supportive to the Emperor
Discuss how her name appearing on coins was a sign of great honor
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the political significance of Nur Jahan in the Mughal administration
activities_projects.evaluation: Understanding of the symbols of power used by Nur Jahan (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Mughal Empire in the 17th Century
activities_projects.objective: Learn about the wealth and the end of the empire
steps: Understand that the empire was very wealthy but many people were poor
Note that Shah Jahan and his nobles spent a lot on salaries and goods
Identify that in the late 17th century, many governors became independent
Name two provinces that became independent (Hyderabad and Awadh)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the contrast between Mughal prosperity and the poverty of the common people
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of why the empire began to break into smaller kingdoms (10 marks)
